headline: FIRST-BASELINE assessment for Clearview AI (rotation queue, never previously assessed; published rank 48, composite 10.9, Critical band). Research confirms baseline pattern but reveals NUANCE: (1) March 2025 federal court approved $51.75M class-action settlement granting class members 23% equity stake — unconventional but materially substantive harm-acknowledgment mechanism (not pure denial); (2) April 2025 Vermont AG REFILED lawsuit alleging facial recognition mapping of Vermont children's faces sold to customers — child harm context; (3) Sep 2025 $10M DHS contract for child-predator identification — claims protective use; (4) Founder/business model continues facial-recognition scraping without consent, NOYB criminal complaint pending. The published score is broadly correct as Critical-band; settlement equity feature (positive) and ongoing child-harm context (negative) approximately offset. Net assessment: composite 11.6 vs published 10.9 — sub-threshold delta of +0.7 pts; band remains Critical. Confirmation, no proposal applied.
key_evidence: March 2025: Federal Court (N.D. Illinois) approved $51.75M class-action BIPA settlement granting 23% equity stake to class — unconventional but a meaningful, court-validated harm-acknowledgment mechanism (University of Miami Law Review).
April 2025: Vermont AG Charity Clark REFILED lawsuit alleging Clearview maps Vermonters' faces, including children, without knowledge, and sells data — recurring CSAM-adjacent child-harm context.
September 2025: Clearview signed $10M DHS contract — largest US federal contract — DHS claims for child-predator identification only.
NOYB criminal complaint pending against Clearview for facial recognition scraping; multiple EU regulatory actions (Italy, France, UK, Greece, Hungary GDPR penalties).
Business model continues: scraping public web images for biometric template database without consent, no opt-out mechanism.

evidence.source: University of Miami Law Review March 2025
evidence.finding: Federal court approved equity-based settlement: 23% equity stake to class members, $51.75M estimated payout — first such resolution in BIPA litigation.
Vermont AG refiles lawsuit alleging Clearview maps Vermonters' faces (including children) without knowledge and sells data to customers.
Sep 2025 $10M DHS contract — largest US federal contract; DHS claims use limited to child-predator identification.
Criminal complaint filed against Clearview AI for facial recognition scraping; multiple EU GDPR enforcement actions ongoing.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1.5 (unchanged). No structured suffering-detection mechanism for those whose faces are scraped without consent.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1.5 (unchanged). Business model fundamentally externalizes affected populations.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1.5 (unchanged). DHS child-predator-identification framing claims protective use but Vermont AG suit demonstrates simultaneous harm to children.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 (unchanged). Vermont children, immigrants, protesters disproportionately surveilled without consent.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.0 (unchanged). B5 Consent Orientation: founder-stated business model is consent-free scraping; floor-level B5 sustained.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.7 (+0.2). AB1 Harm Acknowledgment partially elevated by acceptance of equity-based class settlement (court-supervised); offset by Vermont AG refiling and ongoing EU regulatory actions. AB5 Reparative Action: equity stake is innovative but value is contingent on Clearview itself profiting.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1.5 (unchanged). No structural critique of own surveillance model.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 (unchanged). I1 Consistency Under Pressure: continues scraping despite multiple jurisdictional bans (Italy, France, UK).

negativeSignals: Vermont AG refiled child-harm lawsuit; ongoing EU GDPR enforcement; NOYB criminal complaint pending; consent-free scraping business model continues.
positiveSignals: Court-approved equity-based class settlement (innovative harm-acknowledgment); $10M DHS contract framed as child-protection (claimed protective use, not validated independently).
sector_context: Clearview operates in similar high-harm cluster as xAI/Grok (CSAM context) and Palantir (surveillance/government). Distinct from those two: court-validated harm-acknowledgment mechanism present (settlement equity) lifts ACC slightly above absolute floor.
open_actions_resolved: First-baseline confirmed at Critical band (rank 48 cluster)
Equity-based settlement captured as ACC partial-positive evidence
open_actions_carried: Re-queue Clearview AI for second rotation cycle to monitor (1) Vermont AG outcome (2) DHS contract child-protection use validation (3) further EU GDPR actions
Monitor for any voluntary opt-out or consent mechanism announcements
